summary: Fair‑quality recording of a middling ’95 show, highlighted by a stellar Bird Song and a soaring Estimated Prophet amid uneven set performance.
review: The tape captures an uneven 1995 concert that swings between bright moments – a “tremendous” Bird Song, a “stunning” Estimated Prophet and a solid Touch of Grey opener – and lackluster stretches such as a disappointing Wharf Rat. Reviewers note no obvious sound‑board or audience issues, so the audio is assumed to be merely adequate. Overall the performance feels uneven, earning mixed reactions from listeners.

ai_rating.rationale: Many low‑star reviews point to a subpar show, but several reviewers highlight strong songs; recording quality is unspecified, so the rating reflects an average musical performance with uncertain audio fidelity.

recording_quality.technical_notes: No reviewer mentioned soundboard, audience mix, or clarity; therefore recording quality cannot be judged beyond being passable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set is described as uneven – strong jams and a great opener are offset by misfires, forgotten lyrics, and overall “cylinders misfiring,” leading to an inconsistent concert experience.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia delivers tasty, Sharrock‑style lines in the Bird Song jam but forgets half of Touch of Grey's lyrics; he also rallies on Estimated Prophet.
